wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the PaiAgent-LangGraph4J project. Cover environment requirements including Java 21+, Node.js 18+, MySQL 8.0+, and Maven 3.8+. Provide step-by-step installation instructions for both backend and frontend services. Document database setup with initialization scripts and connection configuration. Include quick start guide with default login credentials and basic workflow creation walkthrough. Explain the development workflow from cloning the repository to running the first successful execution. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for initial configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the frontend application structure and configuration. Document the React application entry point in main.tsx, routing configuration using React Router DOM, and Ant Design internationalization setup. Explain the Vite build configuration, development server setup, and environment variable handling. Cover the application initialization process, global styling setup, and component provider configuration. Include package.json dependencies, build scripts, and development workflow. Address TypeScript configuration, ESLint setup, and Tailwind CSS integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Spring Boot application structure and configuration. Detail the main application entry point, package organization, and component scanning configuration. Explain the web configuration setup including CORS handling, static resource serving, and interceptor registration. Document the application startup process, bean initialization, and configuration loading mechanisms. Cover security interceptors, request processing pipeline, and middleware integration patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Engine Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core workflow engine architecture. Explain the LangGraphWorkflowEngine implementation as the primary execution engine, detailing its integration with LangGraph4j for stateful workflow execution. Document the engine selection strategy, interface implementations, and execution lifecycle management. Cover the workflow execution flow from initialization through completion, including error handling and state management. Include practical examples of engine configuration, execution monitoring, and performance considerations. Address the relationship between different engine types and selection criteria.

wiki_catalogs.name: NodeExecutor Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the NodeExecutor interface. Explain the core contract for all node executors including the execute method signature, input/output parameter handling, and progress callback mechanism. Document the default implementation of the progress callback and how it integrates with the execution event system. Cover the getSupportedNodeType method and its role in node type registration. Include examples of implementing custom node executors and best practices for error handling and result formatting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation covering the complete database structure and table relationships. Detail the workflow table structure with its primary key, foreign key constraints, and indexes. Document the execution_record table for tracking workflow executions including status tracking and audit fields. Explain the node_definition table for reusable node templates and their configuration storage. Include database migration strategies, schema evolution patterns, and version management approaches. Document indexing strategies for optimal query performance and data integrity constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed backend configuration documentation covering the Spring Boot application.yml settings. Document database connection configuration, LLM provider API keys and endpoints, security settings including JWT configuration, file storage setup with MinIO, and logging configuration. Explain the Maven pom.xml dependencies and build configuration. Include environment-specific property overrides, profile-based configurations, and production-ready settings. Provide examples of common configuration scenarios and troubleshooting guides for connection issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Local Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive local development environment setup documentation. Detail prerequisites including Java 21, Node.js, MySQL, and Maven requirements. Document step-by-step installation procedures for both backend and frontend components. Explain environment variable configuration, database setup, and initial project bootstrapping. Include IDE configuration recommendations, debugging setup, and development server startup procedures. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps. Provide verification steps to confirm successful development environment configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Workflow Management endpoints. Document the CRUD operations for workflows including POST /api/workflows for creating workflows, PUT /api/workflows/{id} for updating workflows, DELETE /api/workflows/{id} for deleting workflows, GET /api/workflows/{id} for retrieving workflow details, and GET /api/workflows for listing all workflows. Include detailed request/response schemas, validation rules, authentication requirements, and error handling. Provide practical examples with sample JSON payloads and expected responses. Document the WorkflowRequest and WorkflowResponse DTO structures with field descriptions and validation constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed build process documentation covering both backend and frontend compilation. Document Maven build commands for the Spring Boot backend including dependency resolution, compilation, testing, and packaging phases. Explain frontend build configuration using Vite including development and production builds, asset optimization, and bundle generation. Include environment-specific build profiles, optimization flags for production builds, and artifact generation locations. Provide step-by-step build instructions for local development and CI/CD environments. Document build troubleshooting, dependency conflicts resolution, and build performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for integrating new LLM providers into the PaiAgent system. Explain the AbstractLLMNodeExecutor base class architecture and its key extension points including getNodeType() method implementation, configuration extraction, and response handling. Document the step-by-step process for creating custom LLM node executors by extending AbstractLLMNodeExecutor. Include detailed examples from existing implementations like OpenAINodeExecutor, DeepSeekNodeExecutor, and QwenNodeExecutor showing proper configuration handling, API integration patterns, and error management. Cover ChatClientFactory integration, authentication setup, prompt processing, and output formatting. Provide testing strategies for new LLM integrations and deployment considerations for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the PaiAgent system. Describe the high-level system architecture including frontend React layer, backend Spring Boot application, workflow engine layer, AI model integration layer, and data storage layer. Document the layered architecture pattern with clear component boundaries and data flow between layers. Explain the core design decisions including the use of LangGraph4j for stateful workflows, Spring AI framework for LLM integration, and ReactFlow for visualization. Include system context diagrams showing component interactions and integration patterns. Address scalability considerations, performance characteristics, and technology stack rationale.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the main page components including EditorPage, LoginPage, and MainPage. Document the EditorPage workflow editor implementation with React Flow integration, drag-and-drop functionality, and node manipulation. Explain the LoginPage authentication flow, form validation, and state management. Cover the MainPage layout structure and navigation patterns. Include component props, state management, event handling, and integration with backend APIs. Address responsive design considerations and user experience patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Controller Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the REST API controller layer implementing the MVC pattern. Document all controller classes including WorkflowController for workflow CRUD operations, ExecutionController for workflow execution management, AuthController for authentication endpoints, and NodeDefinitionController for node configuration management. Explain request mapping annotations, parameter binding, response handling, and exception management. Cover HTTP status codes, error response formats, and API versioning strategies. Include authentication decorators, request validation, and security considerations for each endpoint.

wiki_catalogs.name: DAG Parsing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) parsing system. Explain the topology sorting algorithms used for workflow graph processing, cycle detection mechanisms, and dependency resolution. Document the parsing logic for workflow configurations, edge validation, and node ordering. Include examples of valid and invalid workflow structures, parsing error handling, and performance optimization techniques. Address the relationship between parsed DAG structure and execution planning.

wiki_catalogs.name: NodeExecutorFactory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the NodeExecutorFactory pattern implementation. Explain how the factory creates appropriate node executors based on node type configuration. Document the registration mechanism for different node types, the lookup strategy, and dynamic executor instantiation. Cover factory initialization, executor caching strategies, and error handling for unsupported node types. Include examples of adding new node types to the factory and integration with the workflow engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed entity documentation covering the three core data models: Workflow entity for storing workflow configurations and JSON-based flow data, ExecutionRecord entity for tracking execution history with status and timing information, and NodeDefinition entity for managing reusable node templates. Document field definitions, data types, annotations, and validation rules. Explain the MyBatis-Plus annotations including @TableName, @TableId, @TableLogic for soft deletion, and automatic field filling. Include entity lifecycle management, business rule enforcement, and data transformation patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend configuration documentation covering Vite build configuration, TypeScript settings, and Tailwind CSS setup. Document environment variables, proxy configurations for API requests, build optimization settings, and development server configuration. Explain the package.json dependencies and scripts, TypeScript compiler options, and CSS framework configuration. Include deployment configuration for different environments, asset optimization settings, and browser compatibility requirements. Provide guidance on customizing the build process and troubleshooting common frontend build issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Coding Standards & Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Establish comprehensive coding standards and development guidelines for the project. Document Java coding conventions including package structure, class naming, method naming, and code formatting rules. Detail TypeScript/JavaScript standards for frontend development including component structure, state management patterns, and React best practices. Explain architectural patterns used throughout the codebase such as Factory Pattern, Observer Pattern, and Repository Pattern. Include code review guidelines, naming conventions, and documentation requirements. Address performance considerations, security best practices, and maintainability principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: Execution APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for Execution Management endpoints. Document the workflow execution initiation endpoint POST /api/executions with ExecutionRequest payload containing workflow ID and input parameters. Explain the execution monitoring capabilities including real-time progress updates and execution status tracking. Detail the ExecutionResponse structure with execution ID, status, and result data. Document the ExecutionEvent system for streaming execution progress and debug information. Include examples of execution lifecycle, error handling scenarios, and best practices for monitoring long-running executions. Address authentication requirements and rate limiting considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive production deployment documentation. Document containerization strategies using Docker including multi-stage builds, image optimization, and container orchestration with Kubernetes. Explain environment configuration management through application.yml properties, environment variables, and externalized configuration. Cover database migration procedures including schema updates, data migrations, and rollback strategies. Include load balancing setup, SSL/TLS configuration, and reverse proxy setup. Document deployment automation using CI/CD pipelines, blue-green deployments, and rolling updates. Address security hardening including secrets management, network policies, and access controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Node Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for creating custom node types beyond LLM providers. Explain the NodeExecutor interface contract and implementation requirements for developing specialized node types. Document the NodeExecutorFactory pattern for registering and managing custom node executors. Provide step-by-step guides for implementing different node types including InputNodeExecutor, OutputNodeExecutor, and TTSNodeExecutor as reference examples. Cover configuration schema requirements, input/output parameter handling, and integration with the workflow execution engine. Include examples of non-LLM node types such as audio processing, data transformation, and external API integration nodes. Address testing methodologies for custom node types and best practices for maintaining compatibility with the existing workflow system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive frontend documentation covering the React-based user interface architecture. Document the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx entry point through page components (EditorPage, LoginPage, MainPage). Explain the React Flow integration for workflow visualization, Zustand state management patterns, and Ant Design component usage. Detail the workflow editor implementation including drag-and-drop functionality, node configuration panels, and real-time debugging capabilities. Cover routing configuration, authentication state management, and API integration patterns. Include component composition patterns, state management strategies, and user interaction flows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Editor
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the workflow editor system built with React Flow. Document the FlowCanvas component implementation including node rendering, edge drawing, zoom controls, and pan functionality. Explain the NodePanel drag-and-drop system for adding nodes to the canvas. Cover the DebugDrawer real-time execution monitoring interface with progress updates and error handling. Include React Flow configuration, custom node types, event handling, and state synchronization. Address user interaction patterns, canvas manipulation, and workflow visualization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the business logic service layer. Detail the service interfaces and implementations including WorkflowService for workflow orchestration, AuthService for user authentication and authorization, NodeDefinitionService for node configuration management, and MinioService for object storage operations. Explain transaction management, business rule enforcement, and service composition patterns. Document dependency injection, service layer testing strategies, and error handling approaches. Cover service method signatures, parameter validation, and return value handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the state management system in the workflow engine. Explain the AgentState integration with LangGraph4j, state initialization, data transformation, and state validation mechanisms. Document the state extraction processes for input/output data, intermediate results, and execution metadata. Cover state persistence strategies, error state handling, and state cleanup procedures. Include practical examples of state manipulation, debugging state issues, and optimizing state performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: AbstractLLMNodeExecutor Base Class
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the AbstractLLMNodeExecutor base class. Explain how it standardizes LLM node behavior across different providers including common parameter handling, prompt processing, and response formatting. Document the abstract methods that subclasses must implement and the utility methods provided by the base class. Cover LLM-specific configurations, streaming response handling, and error propagation patterns. Include guidelines for extending this base class for new LLM providers and maintaining consistency across implementations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the MyBatis-Plus data access layer covering mapper interfaces and data access patterns. Document the WorkflowMapper, ExecutionRecordMapper, and NodeDefinitionMapper interfaces including CRUD operations, custom queries, and pagination support. Explain the MyBatis-Plus configuration, generic service implementations, and automatic SQL generation. Include data access patterns, transaction management, and performance optimization techniques. Document the relationship between entities and their corresponding mappers, including association mapping and lazy loading strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create environment setup documentation covering prerequisites, system requirements, and step-by-step installation procedures. Document required software versions including Java 21, Node.js, MySQL, and MinIO setup. Explain environment variable configuration, database initialization, and initial project setup. Include development environment setup, IDE configuration recommendations, and debugging setup. Cover production environment preparation, security hardening, and performance optimization settings. Provide validation steps to ensure proper configuration and troubleshooting guides for common setup issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extension Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed extension development documentation for adding new LLM providers and custom node types. Explain the AbstractLLMNodeExecutor base class and its extension points for implementing new AI providers. Document the NodeExecutor interface and factory pattern implementation for creating custom node types. Provide step-by-step guides for integrating new LLM providers including configuration requirements, authentication setup, and API integration patterns. Include examples of existing implementations like OpenAINodeExecutor, DeepSeekNodeExecutor, and QwenNodeExecutor as reference patterns. Address testing strategies for custom extensions and deployment considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Authentication endpoints. Document the user login endpoint POST /api/auth/login with LoginRequest containing username/password credentials and returning LoginResponse with authentication tokens. Explain the authentication flow, token generation, and session management. Detail the LoginRequest and LoginResponse DTO structures including field validation and security considerations. Include examples of successful authentication, error responses for invalid credentials, and token usage patterns. Document logout functionality and token refresh mechanisms if implemented. Address security best practices, CSRF protection, and authentication middleware integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed monitoring and logging documentation. Document application logging configuration using Spring Boot logging properties, log levels, and structured logging formats. Explain metrics collection including JVM metrics, application performance indicators, and custom business metrics. Cover distributed tracing setup for workflow execution monitoring and performance analysis. Include alerting configuration for critical system events, error rates, and resource utilization thresholds. Document log aggregation and analysis using ELK stack or similar solutions. Provide monitoring dashboard setup for key performance indicators, workflow execution statistics, and system health checks. Address log rotation, retention policies, and log security considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Schema & Templates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for LLM node configuration schemas and prompt template systems. Explain the LLMNodeConfig data structure and its role in defining node behavior including API URLs, authentication credentials, model parameters, and temperature settings. Document the PromptTemplateService functionality for dynamic prompt construction using input parameters and workflow data. Cover the WorkflowNode model structure and how node configurations are stored and retrieved. Provide examples of configuration schemas for different LLM providers and node types. Include best practices for prompt engineering, parameter validation, and template variable substitution. Address security considerations for credential storage and configuration management. Document the relationship between frontend configuration interfaces and backend processing logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Application
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive backend documentation covering the Spring Boot application architecture. Document the layered architecture including controller layer for REST API endpoints, service layer for business logic, and data access layer using MyBatis-Plus. Explain the MVC pattern implementation, dependency injection configuration, and interceptor setup for authentication. Detail the REST API endpoints for workflow management, execution control, and user authentication. Cover configuration management, database integration, and security implementation. Include error handling strategies, logging patterns, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the Zustand-based state management system. Document the authStore implementation for authentication state, login/logout actions, and token management. Explain the workflowStore for managing workflow editing state, canvas data, and node configurations. Cover state selectors, action creators, and state persistence strategies. Include integration patterns with React components, async state updates, and state synchronization with backend APIs. Address state hydration, middleware usage, and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the data access layer using MyBatis-Plus framework. Document the mapper interfaces, entity classes, and database interaction patterns. Explain the repository pattern implementation, query methods, and dynamic SQL generation. Detail entity relationships, field mappings, and annotation usage including @TableName, @TableId, and @TableField. Cover database configuration, connection pooling, and transaction management. Include pagination support, custom queries, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Graph Builder
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the graph building system. Explain the construction of LangGraph instances from workflow configurations, node registration, edge establishment, and conditional routing setup. Document the graph compilation process, optimization techniques, and runtime graph modification capabilities. Include examples of complex graph structures, branching logic implementation, and graph validation. Address performance considerations for large graphs and memory optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Specific Node Implementations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for all specific node executor implementations. Document each node type including OpenAI, DeepSeek, Qwen, TTS, Input/Output, AIPing, and ZhiPu nodes. Explain the unique characteristics, configuration requirements, parameter handling, and execution logic for each node type. Cover provider-specific settings, API integration patterns, and result processing. Include examples of node configuration, execution scenarios, and troubleshooting common issues for each implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed security configuration documentation covering authentication and authorization setup. Document JWT token configuration, CORS settings, CSRF protection, and security headers. Explain the authentication interceptor implementation, role-based access control, and API security patterns. Cover LLM provider API key security, encrypted storage configuration, and secure communication settings. Include security best practices, vulnerability mitigation strategies, and compliance considerations. Provide guidance on securing sensitive configuration data and monitoring security events.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing strategy documentation covering unit testing, integration testing, and test data management. Document the existing test structure including LangGraphWorkflowEngineTest and basic test patterns. Explain testing methodologies for workflow execution, node execution, and state management components. Detail test data setup, mocking strategies for external dependencies like LLM providers, and test environment configuration. Include guidelines for writing effective unit tests, integration tests, and performance tests. Address continuous integration testing, test coverage requirements, and automated testing workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintenance & Troubleshooting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive maintenance and troubleshooting documentation. Document routine maintenance procedures including database cleanup, log file rotation, cache management, and system updates. Explain performance monitoring and optimization techniques including memory tuning, database query optimization, and workflow execution performance. Cover common operational issues including workflow execution failures, LLM provider connectivity problems, and database connection issues. Include diagnostic procedures for identifying bottlenecks, memory leaks, and performance degradation. Document backup and restore procedures for database and configuration data. Provide escalation procedures for critical incidents and incident response protocols. Address preventive maintenance schedules and system health verification procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the core workflow engine system. Explain the LangGraph4j integration for stateful workflow execution, DAG parsing algorithms for topology sorting and cycle detection, and the node executor factory pattern for dynamic node execution. Document the workflow execution lifecycle, state management mechanisms, and data flow between nodes. Cover the engine selection strategy, execution monitoring capabilities, and error handling patterns. Include practical examples of workflow configuration, execution scenarios, and debugging approaches. Address performance considerations and scalability aspects of the workflow engine.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frontend API integration layer. Document the auth API module for authentication endpoints, token handling, and session management. Explain the workflow API module for CRUD operations on workflows, execution requests, and state synchronization. Cover the request utility functions for HTTP client configuration, error handling, and response transformation. Include authentication headers, error response handling, loading states, and retry mechanisms. Address API versioning, timeout configuration, and offline handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for application configuration and management. Detail the Spring Boot configuration system including application.yml properties, environment-specific configurations, and externalized configuration patterns. Document the Minio configuration for object storage integration, meta object handler for automatic field filling, and custom configuration beans. Explain property binding, configuration validation, and environment variable substitution. Cover logging configuration, database connection settings, and third-party service integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Execution Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the execution monitoring and event system. Explain the event callback mechanism for real-time execution tracking, progress reporting, and error notifications. Document the ExecutionEvent structure, event types, and callback handling patterns. Cover monitoring capabilities for workflow execution, node-level progress tracking, and performance metrics collection. Include examples of implementing custom event handlers, debugging execution issues, and integrating with external monitoring systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debugging & Profiling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed debugging and profiling documentation for development workflow optimization. Document debugging techniques for workflow execution issues, node execution problems, and state management challenges. Explain the DebugDrawer component functionality and real-time execution monitoring capabilities. Detail logging strategies, error tracking, and performance profiling approaches. Include guidelines for debugging LLM provider integrations, workflow state issues, and frontend-backend communication problems. Address performance optimization techniques, memory management, and bottleneck identification. Provide troubleshooting guides for common development issues and production debugging scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Node Execution System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the node execution system. Explain the NodeExecutor interface and factory pattern implementation for dynamic node creation. Document the AbstractLLMNodeExecutor base class and its role in standardizing LLM node behavior across different providers. Detail the specific node implementations including OpenAI, DeepSeek, Qwen, TTS, Input/Output nodes, and custom node development patterns. Cover the execution lifecycle, parameter handling, error propagation, and result formatting. Include guidelines for extending the system with new node types and integrating additional AI providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Persistence
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation covering the database schema and entity relationships. Detail the Workflow entity for storing workflow configurations, ExecutionRecord entity for tracking execution history, and NodeDefinition entity for managing reusable node templates. Document the database table structures, field definitions, primary/foreign key relationships, and indexing strategies. Explain the MyBatis-Plus configuration, mapper interfaces, and data access patterns. Include data validation rules, business constraints, and data lifecycle management. Cover database migration strategies and schema evolution approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all REST endpoints. Document the Workflow Management APIs including CRUD operations for workflows with request/response schemas and authentication requirements. Detail the Execution APIs covering workflow execution initiation, status monitoring, and result retrieval. Explain the Authentication APIs for user login/logout processes. Include HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response formats, error codes, and authentication mechanisms. Provide practical examples with curl commands and SDK usage patterns. Address rate limiting, security considerations, and API versioning strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive configuration documentation covering both backend and frontend setup requirements. Document the Spring Boot application.yml configuration including database connections, LLM provider settings, security configurations, and file storage setup. Explain the frontend Vite configuration, environment variables, and build optimization settings. Detail the MinIO object storage configuration for file handling. Cover production deployment configurations, environment-specific settings, and security hardening measures. wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development documentation for contributors and extension developers. Cover local development environment setup, coding standards, and development workflow. Document the process for adding new LLM providers by extending AbstractLLMNodeExecutor and implementing custom node types. Explain the testing strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and test data management. Detail the code organization principles, naming conventions, and architectural patterns used throughout the codebase. wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation. Cover the build process for both backend and frontend applications including Maven and npm build commands, optimization flags, and artifact generation. Document production deployment strategies including containerization options, environment configuration management, and database migration procedures. Include backup and disaster recovery procedures, security hardening measures, and maintenance schedules. Address troubleshooting common deployment issues and operational best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation addressing common issues and frequently asked questions. Document typical development environment problems including dependency conflicts, port binding issues, and IDE configuration problems. Cover LLM provider integration issues such as API key configuration, network connectivity problems, and quota limitations. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# PaiAgent-LangGraph4J: AI Agent Workflow Platform

## Project Introduction

PaiAgent-LangGraph4J is a comprehensive AI agent workflow platform that combines a modern frontend editor with a robust backend execution engine. The platform enables users to visually design, configure, and execute complex AI agent workflows using multiple Large Language Model (LLM) providers including OpenAI, DeepSeek, and Tongyi Qianwen.

### Purpose Statement
The platform serves as an integrated solution for AI workflow automation, providing both a visual editor for designing agent interactions and a powerful execution engine capable of handling sophisticated multi-step AI processes.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Visual Workflow Design**: Enable intuitive drag-and-drop workflow creation through React-based frontend
- **Multi-Provider Support**: Integrate with various LLM providers through unified interfaces
- **Advanced Execution Engine**: Leverage LangGraph4j for sophisticated stateful AI agent workflows
- **Real-time Monitoring**: Provide debugging capabilities with live execution feedback
- **Flexible Architecture**: Support both simple DAG workflows and complex state machine patterns

### Target Audience
- AI developers and engineers building automated workflows
- Business analysts creating AI-powered business processes
- Technical teams implementing conversational AI solutions
- Organizations requiring customizable AI agent orchestration

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with clear separation between frontend and backend concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[RESTful API Layer]
    B --> C[Business Logic Layer]
    C --> D[Workflow Engine]
    D --> E[LLM Providers]
    D --> F[Storage Layer]
    G[Database MySQL] --> F
    H[MinIO Storage] --> F
    
    subgraph "Frontend Components"
        A1[EditorPage] --> A2[FlowCanvas]
        A2 --> A3[NodePanel]
        A3 --> A4[DebugDrawer]
    end
    
    subgraph "Backend Services"
        B1[WorkflowController] --> B2[WorkflowService]
        B2 --> B3[LangGraphWorkflowEngine]
        B3 --> B4[NodeExecutorFactory]
    end
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture**
- React 18 with TypeScript for type-safe development
- Ant Design for UI components and theming
- Zustand for state management
- React Flow for interactive workflow canvas
- Vite for fast development and build process

**Backend Architecture**
- Spring Boot 3.4.1 for microservice foundation
- MyBatis-Plus for ORM operations
- LangGraph4j integration for advanced workflow execution
- Swagger/OpenAPI for API documentation
- MinIO for object storage integration

### Design Patterns

The platform implements several key design patterns:

- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through MyBatis-Plus
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic node executor creation
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven workflow execution monitoring
- **Command Pattern**: Node-specific execution strategies
- **State Machine Pattern**: Advanced workflow state management

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Frontend
    participant Backend
    participant Engine
    participant LLM
    
    User->>Frontend: Create Workflow
    Frontend->>Backend: POST /api/workflows
    Backend->>Backend: Validate & Store
    Backend-->>Frontend: Workflow Created
    
    User->>Frontend: Execute Workflow
    Frontend->>Backend: POST /api/execution
    Backend->>Engine: Execute Workflow
    Engine->>LLM: Process Nodes
    LLM-->>Engine: Node Results
    Engine-->>Backend: Execution Complete
    Backend-->>Frontend: Execution Response
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/PaiAgentApplication.java` - Spring Boot main application entry point
- Configures MyBatis-Plus scanning and application startup

**Frontend Application**
- `frontend/src/App.tsx` - React application root with routing configuration
- Implements Ant Design internationalization and React Router setup

### Core Modules

**Workflow Engine**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/langgraph/LangGraphWorkflowEngine.java` - Primary execution engine
- Implements WorkflowExecutor interface for LangGraph-based workflows
- Handles state management and event callbacks

**Node Execution System**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/executor/NodeExecutor.java` - Interface defining node execution contracts
- Factory pattern implementation for dynamic executor creation
- Supports multiple node types (LLM, Input, Output, TTS)

**Data Models**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/model/WorkflowConfig.java` - Core workflow configuration structure
- Defines nodes and edges for workflow execution
- JSON-based configuration for flexible workflow definitions

### Configuration Approach

**Backend Configuration**
- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` - Centralized configuration management
- Database connectivity and MyBatis-Plus settings
- LLM provider configurations and API endpoints
- MinIO storage configuration for file handling

**Frontend Configuration**
- `frontend/package.json` - Dependency management and build scripts
- React application configuration with TypeScript support
- Development and production build configurations

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- Spring Boot starters for web and validation
- MyBatis-Plus for database operations
- LangGraph4j for workflow execution
- Spring AI for LLM integrations
- FastJSON2 for JSON processing
- MinIO SDK for object storage

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 18 with React DOM for rendering
- Ant Design UI components and icons
- React Flow for workflow visualization
- Zustand for state management
- Axios for HTTP requests

### Integration Points

**Database Schema**
- `backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql` - Complete database schema definition
- Workflow table for storing visual workflow configurations
- Execution records for tracking workflow runs
- Node definitions for standardized node types

**Authentication Service**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/service/AuthService.java` - Simple token-based authentication
- In-memory token storage for development environment
- Basic username/password validation

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class WorkflowController {
        +createWorkflow()
        +updateWorkflow()
        +deleteWorkflow()
        +getWorkflow()
        +listWorkflows()
    }
    
    class WorkflowService {
        +createWorkflow()
        +updateWorkflow()
        +deleteWorkflow()
        +getWorkflowById()
        +listWorkflows()
    }
    
    class LangGraphWorkflowEngine {
        +execute()
        +executeWithCallback()
        +getEngineType()
    }
    
    class NodeExecutor {
        <<interface>>
        +execute()
        +getSupportedNodeType()
    }
    
    class WorkflowEntity {
        +id: Long
        +name: String
        +flowData: JSON
        +engineType: String
    }
    
    WorkflowController --> WorkflowService
    WorkflowService --> WorkflowEntity
    WorkflowService --> LangGraphWorkflowEngine
    LangGraphWorkflowEngine --> NodeExecutor
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Visual Workflow Editor**
- Drag-and-drop interface for workflow construction
- Real-time validation and error checking
- Node parameter configuration with dynamic forms
- Export/import functionality for workflow sharing

**Multi-Provider LLM Integration**
- Unified interface for OpenAI, DeepSeek, and Tongyi Qianwen
- Configurable API endpoints and authentication
- Parameter templating with node reference support
- Token usage tracking and optimization

**Advanced Execution Engine**
- Support for both simple DAG workflows and complex state machines
- Real-time execution monitoring with progress callbacks
- Comprehensive error handling and recovery mechanisms
- Performance metrics and execution history tracking

### Implementation Highlights

**Frontend Architecture**
- `frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx` - Comprehensive workflow editor with real-time collaboration features
- `frontend/src/components/FlowCanvas.tsx` - Interactive canvas with zoom, pan, and node manipulation
- `frontend/src/store/workflowStore.ts` - Centralized state management for workflow data
- `frontend/src/api/workflow.ts` - RESTful API integration layer

**Backend Processing**
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/langgraph/builder/GraphBuilder.java` - Dynamic graph construction from workflow definitions
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/langgraph/state/StateManager.java` - Advanced state management for complex workflows
- `backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/mapper/ExecutionRecordMapper.java` - Persistent execution tracking and analytics

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Editing: Load Workflow
    Editing --> Validating: Save Changes
    Validating --> Editing: Validation Failed
    Validating --> Ready: Validation Passed
    Ready --> Executing: Run Workflow
    Executing --> Monitoring: Node Started
    Monitoring --> Monitoring: Node Completed
    Monitoring --> Success: All Nodes Done
    Executing --> Failed: Execution Error
    Success --> Idle: Reset
    Failed --> Idle: Reset
```

**Development Tools Integration**
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` - Modern build configuration with hot reload
- `backend/pom.xml` - Maven-based dependency management with Spring AI integration
- ESLint and TypeScript configuration for frontend quality assurance

**Deployment Considerations**
- Docker-compatible configuration for containerized deployment
- Environment variable-based configuration for different environments
- Database migration support through Flyway or Liquibase patterns

---

**Sources:**
- [PaiAgentApplication.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/PaiAgentApplication.java)
- [application.yml](backend/src/main/resources/application.yml)
- [pom.xml](backend/pom.xml)
- [LangGraphWorkflowEngine.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/langgraph/LangGraphWorkflowEngine.java)
- [WorkflowController.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/controller/WorkflowController.java)
- [App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx)
- [WorkflowConfig.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/model/WorkflowConfig.java)
- [NodeExecutor.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/engine/executor/NodeExecutor.java)
- [WorkflowService.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/service/WorkflowService.java)
- [schema.sql](backend/src/main/resources/schema.sql)
- [AuthService.java](backend/src/main/java/com/paiagent/service/AuthService.java)
- [EditorPage.tsx](frontend/src/pages/EditorPage.tsx)
- [FlowCanvas.tsx](frontend/src/components/FlowCanvas.tsx)
- [package.json](frontend/package.json)

</blog>
